mission.title: Install and Configure Confidential Containers on Kubernetes
mission.description: Confidential Containers is an open-source community project that leverages Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to enhance the security of containers and protect sensitive data in cloud-native applications. Installing it allows you to enforce application security requirements while maintaining transparency in deploying unmodified containers.

steps.title: Add the Confidential Containers Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Helm chart repository for Confidential Containers to your Helm client. This will allow you to install the necessary components easily.

```bash
helm repo add confidential-containers https://confidential-containers.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Confidential Containers Helm chart
steps.description:
Now, install the Confidential Containers chart with the specified version and create a namespace for it.

```bash
helm install confidential-containers confidential-containers/confidential-containers --version v0.17.0 --namespace confidential --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the pods are running successfully in the confidential namespace.

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace confidential
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Confidential Containers deployment to ensure optimal performance and resource utilization.

```bash
kubectl set resources deployment confidential-containers --namespace confidential --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for security
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to ensure that the Confidential Containers can operate with the least privilege necessary.

```bash
kubectl create role confidential-containers-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace=confidential
kubectl create rolebinding confidential-containers-rolebinding --role=confidential-containers-role --serviceaccount=confidential:default --namespace=confidential
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Confidential Containers release using Helm.

```bash
helm uninstall confidential-containers --namespace confidential
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with the release.

```bash
kubectl delete crd confidentialcontainers.confidentialcontainers.io
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the namespace used for the installation to clean up all associated resources.

```bash
kubectl delete namespace confidential
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of the deployment.

```bash
kubectl get all --namespace confidential -o yaml > backup-confidential-containers.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts available.

```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Confidential Containers installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the installation to the latest version available in the Helm repository.

```bash
helm upgrade confidential-containers confidential-containers/confidential-containers --version v0.17.0 --namespace confidential
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure they are running the new version.

```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace confidential
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.

```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace confidential
``` 
Ensure that there are enough resources available in the cluster or adjust the resource requests in your deployment.

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see CrashLoopBackOff errors, check the logs of the affected pod to diagnose the issue.

```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace confidential
``` 
Look for any application errors or misconfigurations.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, verify that the service is correctly configured and the endpoints are available.

```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace confidential
kubectl describe svc <service-name> --namespace confidential
``` 
Ensure that the service type and ports are correctly set.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Confidential Containers will result in all pods running without errors in the 'confidential' namespace. You should also be able to access the deployed services as intended.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add confidential-containers https://confidential-containers.github.io/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment confidential-containers --namespace confidential --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

kubectl create role confidential-containers-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace=confidential
kubectl create rolebinding confidential-containers-rolebinding --role=confidential-containers-role --serviceaccount=confidential:default --namespace=confidential

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
